commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens `isShortTermMemoryPath()` so `memory/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md` entries are treated like short-term memory paths.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main filters timestamp-suffixed and slugged short-term memory paths before the dreaming stats counters run. I did not execute the gateway handler in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for the incomplete path contract and real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not repair it automatically while contributor proof is still insufficient.

target.title: fix(doctor): match timestamp-suffixed daily memory files in isShortTermMemoryPath (fixes #90896)
target.body:
**Summary**

Updates `isShortTermMemoryPath()` regex to match daily memory files with timestamp suffixes (e.g. `memory/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md`), fixing underreported dreaming stats in the WebUI Dreams tab. 1 file, 1 line changed.

**Root Cause**

The `isShortTermMemoryPath()` function in `src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.ts` uses a regex at line 353 that only matches `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. When the dreaming recall store promotes entries, it creates files with paths like `memory/2026-05-28-1503.md`, which include a `-HHMM` timestamp suffix. These timestamp-suffixed files are skipped by the regex filter, causing `promotedToday`, `shortTermCount`, and `promotedTotal` to show incorrect (zero/undercounted) values.

- **Invariant violated**: `isShortTermMemoryPath()` should recognize all valid short-term memory file paths
- **Code path**: `doctor.memory.status` → iterate recall store entries → `isShortTermMemoryPath()` filter (line 524) → entries with timestamp-suffixed paths are discarded
- **Why this is a root-cause fix**: The regex is the single gatekeeper for determining which paths count as valid memory entries. No other code path compensates for the skipped files.
- **Sibling audit**: The `session-corpus` regex at line 357 already handles multiple extensions (`md|txt`). This fix applies the same flexibility principle to the daily memory path regex.

**Verification**

```
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts
```

- 2 test files, 60 tests passed (exit 0)
- `pnpm build` passes cleanly

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `isShortTermMemoryPath()` regex did not match daily memory files with `-HHMM` timestamp suffixes; now it does.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw main @ f4a5e5762e, pnpm 11.2.2

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node -e "
const re = /(?:^|\/)memory\/(\d{4})-(\d{2})-(\d{2})(?:-\d{4})?\.md$/;
console.log('memory/2026-05-28.md =>', re.test('memory/2026-05-28.md'));
console.log('memory/2026-05-28-1503.md =>', re.test('memory/2026-05-28-1503.md'));
console.log('memory/2025-12-01-0930.md =>', re.test('memory/2025-12-01-0930.md'));
"
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
memory/2026-05-28.md => true
memory/2026-05-28-1503.md => true
memory/2025-12-01-0930.md => true
```

Before fix, the same test produced:
```
memory/2026-05-28-1503.md => false
memory/2025-12-01-0930.md => false
```

Source change: [src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.ts:353](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.ts#L353) — `(?:-\d{4})?` added to optionally match `-HHMM` timestamp suffix.

**Observed result after fix**: All timestamp-suffixed paths now match correctly. No regressions — plain `YYYY-MM-DD.md` paths continue to match as before. `pnpm test` passes 60/60. `pnpm build` exits 0.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end WebUI Dreams tab rendering with real recall store data containing timestamp-suffixed entries (requires running gateway with a populated recall store).

**Review Findings Addressed**

N/A — autoreview clean, no bot findings to address.

**Regression Test Plan**

```bash
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts
```

The existing doctor test suite covers the dreaming status endpoint that calls `isShortTermMemoryPath()`.

**Merge Risk**

Low. The change adds an optional `(?:-\d{4})?` group to an existing regex — all previously matching paths continue to match. The regex is only used in one function in one file. No API surface, config, or external dependency changes.
target.author: zenglingbiao

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
